question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, when must an intermediary provide the compliance certificate to SEBI?

answer.text: On the 31st March of each year
On the 1st January of each year
Within 30 days of the end of each financial year
On the 1st April of each year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An intermediary shall provide the Board with a certificate of its compliance officer on the 1st April of each year certifying compliance with all obligations and eligibility criteria. **The Trap:** 31st March is the financial year end, but the compliance certificate is due on 1st April, the start of the new financial year. **Cross-Exam Context:** Annual compliance certification requirements are tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Mark 1st April as a key compliance deadline - the certificate must cover compliance status and all SEBI-prescribed disclosures.
